Researcher · Information Science

Gongpu Chen (陈功谱)

I am a postdoctoral researcher at Imperial College London, working with Prof. Deniz Gündüz. I obtained my PhD degree from The Chinese University of Hong Kong in 2023, under the supervision of Prof. Soung Chang Liew. Prior to that, I obtained my Master’s degree from Southeast University in 2019 and my Bachelor’s degree from the University of Electronic Science and Technology of China in 2016.

Research Associate
Imperial College London

Google Scholar CV / Résumé

Research

My research interests lie at the intersection of communication, control, and machine learning. At the core of my work is a fundamental question: How does the interplay between information and control/decision-making shape the performance, efficiency, and robustness of intelligent cyber-physical systems? I explore this question from both communication and control perspectives, drawing on tools from information theory, control theory, Markov decision processes, reinforcement/machine learning, and optimization.

🔒
PrivacyEdge
A framework for privacy-preserving inference at the network edge using secure multi-party computation and differential privacy.
Learn more →
⚡
FedBench
An open benchmark suite for evaluating federated learning algorithms across heterogeneous data distributions and communication constraints.
Learn more →
🧠
SketchML
Randomized sketching methods for training neural networks with sub-linear memory complexity. Part of my PhD thesis.
Learn more →
📡
GossipOpt
Decentralized optimization over gossip communication networks, with provable convergence guarantees under adversarial link failures.
Learn more →
